action.reasoning: Observation: Training crashed on task 1 with RuntimeError (4D vs 5D tensors in cat for dataset_images/mem_images). This matches the truncated train.py's flawed memory handling (appending 4D per-class slices to list then torch.stack produces 5D). Predict.py also failed previously on checkpoint key ('model_state_dict' vs raw state_dict). Val acc ~0.07 after task 0 is expected (10-class model on 100-class val); best remains 0.465 from iter 2 but consolidation in iter 8 reached val~0.50 before errors. Curves are broken due to missing early logs. Analysis: Root cause is inconsistent memory buffer (list of 4D vs 3D tensors) + save/load mismatch; replay helps but buggy impl limits gains vs naive forgetting (0.019). Decision: Fully rewrite clean train.py using tensor-based memory (torch.cat on 4D images/labels, balanced per-class selection via meta['classes_per_task'] and torch.where, no lists for stack issues), Adam(lr=0.001), memory_per_task=400 (~40/cls), epochs_per_task=6, add post-training consolidation (4 epochs, lr=5e-4 on memory only), log every 30 steps + val per epoch + final ckpt with 'model_state_dict' key. Update predict.py to be robust (handles both checkpoint formats, batch inference, same transforms). Keep model.py. Run training then submit_eval to get >0.47 avg_acc baseline.
